lede: A look at April 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Roman Herzog and Julio Ángel Fernández, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement
events.desc: Agents with the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement raid a slaughterhouse in Tennessee, detaining nearly 100 undocumented Hispanic workers in one of the largest workplace raids in the history of the United States.

events.title: 2010 North-West Frontier Province attacks
events.desc: Up to 50 people are killed and another 100 injured in two militant suicide bombings and attacks in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province of Pakistan: the first on an Awami National Party rally in Timergara; the second on the U.S. Consulate in Peshawar.

events.title: Japan
events.desc: In Japan, the Akashi Kaikyō Bridge opens to traffic, becoming the longest bridge span in the world.

events.title: People's Armed Police
events.desc: The People's Armed Police is officially founded.

events.title: China
events.desc: In China, the April Fifth Movement leads to the Tiananmen Incident.

events.title: Carrie (novel)
events.desc: Carrie, the first novel by American author Stephen King, is published for the first time with a print run of 30,000 copies.

events.title: Buddhist Uprising
events.desc: During the Buddhist Uprising, South Vietnamese Prime Minister Nguyễn Cao Kỳ personally attempts to lead the capture of the restive city of Đà Nẵng before backing down.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Adolf Hitler issues Fuhrer Directive No. 41 summarizing Case Blue, including the German Sixth Army's planned assault on Stalingrad.

events.title: 1902 Ibrox disaster
events.desc: A stand box collapses at Ibrox Park (now Ibrox Stadium) in Glasgow, Scotland, which led to the deaths of 25 and injuries to more than 500 supporters during an international association football match between Scotland and England.

events.title: George Washington
events.desc: United States President George Washington exercises his authority to veto a bill, the first time this power is used in the United States.

births.name: Roman Herzog
births.line: German lawyer and politician, 7th President of Germany

births.name: Quade Cooper
births.line: New Zealand-Australian rugby player and boxer

births.name: Nadar
births.line: French photographer, caricaturist, journalist, novelist and balloonist

deaths.text: Chiang Kai-shek, Chinese general and politician, 1st President of the Republic of China

observances.text: Christian feast day: Agape, Chionia, and Irene
